want: call all 4 prune functions after collectPhaseSnapshots and before moving the workflow to workflow_history
so_that: state.json is pruned on the primary path at every workflow completion, keeping the file lean
acceptance_criteria: AC-001-01: pruneSkillUsageLog(state, 50) called after snapshots, before history move
AC-001-02: pruneCompletedPhases(state, []) called with empty protected-phases
AC-001-03: pruneHistory(state, 100, 200) called
AC-001-04: pruneWorkflowHistory(state, 50, 200) called
AC-001-05: workflow_history entry contains snapshots/metrics collected BEFORE pruning

want: reset all transient runtime fields to their null/empty state at workflow completion
so_that: stale data from prior workflows does not leak into subsequent runs and confuse hooks
acceptance_criteria: AC-002-01: pending_escalations set to []
AC-002-02: pending_delegation set to null
AC-002-03: current_phase set to null
AC-002-04: active_agent set to null
AC-002-05: phases set to {}
AC-002-06: blockers set to []

want: provide a single clearTransientFields(state) function that resets all transient fields in one call
so_that: cleanup logic is centralized and callable from both the orchestrator finalize path and the enforcer fallback
acceptance_criteria: AC-003-01: pending_escalations set to []
AC-003-02: pending_delegation set to null
AC-003-03: current_phase set to null
AC-003-04: active_agent set to null
AC-003-05: phases set to {}
AC-003-06: blockers set to []
AC-003-07: returns the mutated state object
AC-003-08: durable fields are NOT modified

want: FIFO retention limits updated to match real-world usage patterns (50/100/50)
so_that: pruning keeps an adequate history window without over-retaining stale data
acceptance_criteria: AC-004-01: pruneSkillUsageLog default is 50 (up from 20)
AC-004-02: pruneHistory default is 100 (up from 50)
AC-004-03: pruneWorkflowHistory default is 50 (unchanged)
AC-004-04: arrays at or below cap are not modified

want: call clearTransientFields(state) after its existing prune function calls
so_that: the fallback path is as comprehensive as the orchestrator primary path, ensuring transient fields are always cleaned
acceptance_criteria: AC-005-01: clearTransientFields called after pruning on completed workflow detection
AC-005-02: all 6 transient fields are reset in the written state

want: have explicit instructions in MODE: finalize telling me to invoke pruning with specific function names and retention limits
so_that: the prompt-driven agent knows exactly what to call and in what order, since the orchestrator is not executable code
acceptance_criteria: AC-006-01: explicit step listing all 5 function calls (4 prune + clearTransientFields)
AC-006-02: order specified: collectPhaseSnapshots FIRST, then prune, then workflow_history move
AC-006-03: retention limits specified: skill_usage_log 50, history 100, workflow_history 50

want: older workflow_history entries (beyond most recent 10) to have phase_snapshots compacted to phase+summary only
so_that: state.json stays lean over time by reducing per-entry size for historical records rarely inspected in detail
acceptance_criteria: AC-007-01: entries beyond most recent 10 have phase_snapshots compacted to [{phase, summary}]
AC-007-02: most recent 10 entries preserve full phase_snapshots
AC-007-03: compacted entries have _compacted: true flag

want: older workflow_history entries (beyond most recent 5) to have git_branch compacted to {name, status} only
so_that: recent entries keep full git detail while older entries shed unused metadata
acceptance_criteria: AC-008-01: entries beyond most recent 5 have git_branch compacted to {name, status}
AC-008-02: most recent 5 entries preserve full git_branch

want: run a one-time migration on first workflow init after deployment that seeds the archive from existing workflow_history and then prunes state.json
so_that: the archive has a complete picture from day one and state.json is immediately brought under control without waiting for the next finalize
acceptance_criteria: AC-009-01: migration runs on first workflow init after deployment
AC-009-02: seedArchiveFromHistory called BEFORE FIFO pruning
AC-009-03: legacy git_branch objects transformed to branch+outcome
AC-009-04: full phase_snapshots compacted to phase_summary format
AC-009-05: missing source_id/slug handled gracefully (no error)
AC-009-06: FIFO caps applied after seeding (50/100/50)
AC-009-07: stale transient fields cleared if active_workflow is null
AC-009-08: migration is idempotent (pruning_migration_completed flag)

want: archive completed and cancelled workflow records to state-archive.json with a multi-key index whenever active_workflow becomes null
so_that: workflow data moves out of state.json into a separate archive, enabling the hot/cold data split that keeps state.json lean
acceptance_criteria: AC-010-01: compact record appended to state-archive.json on finalize
AC-010-02: index updated with both source_id and slug entries
AC-010-03: archive file created with version 1 on first write
AC-010-04: archive write failure logs warning, does not roll back state.json
AC-010-05: re-work scenario maps identifier to multiple record positions
AC-010-06: cancelled workflows get outcome 'cancelled' with reason from cancellation_reason
AC-010-07: completed workflows get reason null

want: provide an appendToArchive(record, projectId) function that reads/creates the archive file, appends a record, updates the multi-key index, and writes the file
so_that: archive writes are encapsulated in a single function with fail-safe behavior that any caller can use
acceptance_criteria: AC-011-01: record appended and index updated when archive exists
AC-011-02: new archive created with version 1 when file does not exist
AC-011-03: both source_id and slug added as index keys
AC-011-04: file read/write errors caught, warning logged, no throw
AC-011-05: function exported as appendToArchive

want: look up past workflow records by issue identifier in the archive
so_that: I can supplement GitHub/BACKLOG answers with internal process data like workflow duration, phases completed, and branch name
acceptance_criteria: AC-012-01: exact match on index returns {found: true, records: [...]}
AC-012-02: no match returns {found: false, records: []}
AC-012-03: missing archive file returns {found: false, records: []}
AC-012-04: multiple matches sorted by completed_at descending
AC-012-05: any error returns {found: false, records: []} without throwing
notes: Deferred: GitHub/BACKLOG already answers the basic question. Archive data accumulates via write path; read path built later.

want: detect an orphaned active_workflow from a previous session and archive it with outcome 'abandoned' before initializing the new workflow
so_that: stale transient fields do not leak into new workflows (correctness fix) and a record of what happened is preserved
acceptance_criteria: AC-013-01: orphaned active_workflow detected before new init proceeds
AC-013-02: archive record has outcome 'abandoned'
AC-013-03: phase_summary reflects progress; incomplete phases get status 'abandoned'
AC-013-04: metrics.total_duration_minutes calculated from started_at to now
AC-013-05: clearTransientFields called after archive write (or failure)
AC-013-06: one-line notification displayed: 'Archived abandoned workflow for {source_id}'
AC-013-07: archive failure does not block cleanup (fail-open)

want: provide a resolveArchivePath(projectId) function that resolves the absolute path to state-archive.json, mirroring resolveStatePath() for monorepo support
so_that: archive files are routed to the correct per-project directory in monorepo mode, preventing cross-project contamination
acceptance_criteria: AC-015-01: single-project mode returns {projectRoot}/.isdlc/state-archive.json
AC-015-02: monorepo mode with explicit projectId returns correct project path
AC-015-03: monorepo mode without projectId auto-detects from cwd
AC-015-04: resolveArchivePath produces same directory as resolveStatePath, different filename
AC-015-05: uses cached getProjectRoot() (no redundant filesystem traversal)
AC-015-06: exported as resolveArchivePath

want: provide a seedArchiveFromHistory(workflowHistory) function that transforms legacy workflow_history entries to archive record format and appends each via appendToArchive()
so_that: the one-time migration can seed the archive with all 18 historical workflows, giving a complete picture from day one
acceptance_criteria: AC-014-01: legacy git_branch objects transformed to branch name + outcome status
AC-014-02: full phase_snapshots compacted to phase_summary format
AC-014-03: missing source_id entries still appended, indexed by slug only
AC-014-04: entries missing both source_id and slug appended with no index entries
AC-014-05: individual entry failures skipped, processing continues
